text: After the introduction of four trocars, the laparoscopic exploration reveals signs of initial fecal peritonitis within the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon incises the peritoneum, identifies the sigmoidal vessels, and isolates them before sectioning between hemlock clips.
The surgeon opens the left paracolic gutter, freeing the descending colon in preparation for colostomy.
The surgeon places the resected specimen into an iron bag to prevent fecal contamination, followed by a thorough irrigation of the entire abdominal cavity.
